description:
## What

The libraries management page where users can view, add, and remove web component libraries from their registry. Plus a parameterized single-library dashboard.

## Deliverables

1. **Libraries index page** (apps/admin/src/app/libraries/page.tsx):
   - Card grid showing all registered libraries with name, prefix, source badge (Local, CDN, npm), component count, last scored date, health grade
   - Add Library button with modal form: name, source type selector, conditional fields (cemPath for local, packageName and version for CDN or npm), prefix
   - Delete button per library (disabled for default)
   - Score Now and Resolve CEM action buttons

2. **Single library dashboard** (apps/admin/src/app/libraries/[id]/page.tsx):
   - Reuse existing health scoring UI components (ScoreComparison, dimension breakdown)
   - Parameterized to work with any library from the registry
   - For local: full 17-dimension scoring view
   - For CDN: CEM-only scoring with clear CEM-Only Analysis badge
   - Component list with individual health grades

3. **Navigation** — Add Libraries link to the admin sidebar between existing nav items

## Acceptance Criteria

- Adding a CDN library (e.g. Shoelace) works end-to-end: add, resolve CEM, score, view dashboard
- Adding a local library with valid CEM path works
- Default hx-library is always present and cannot be deleted
- Existing health and mcp routes continue to work unchanged
- TypeScript compiles with zero errors
- Responsive layout, consistent with existing admin dark theme
title: M3: Libraries Management UI & Navigation

descriptionHistory.description:
## What

The libraries management page where users can view, add, and remove web component libraries from their registry. Plus a parameterized single-library dashboard.

## Deliverables

1. **Libraries index page** (apps/admin/src/app/libraries/page.tsx):
   - Card grid showing all registered libraries with name, prefix, source badge (Local, CDN, npm), component count, last scored date, health grade
   - Add Library button with modal form: name, source type selector, conditional fields (cemPath for local, packageName and version for CDN or npm), prefix
   - Delete button per library (disabled for default)
   - Score Now and Resolve CEM action buttons

2. **Single library dashboard** (apps/admin/src/app/libraries/[id]/page.tsx):
   - Reuse existing health scoring UI components (ScoreComparison, dimension breakdown)
   - Parameterized to work with any library from the registry
   - For local: full 17-dimension scoring view
   - For CDN: CEM-only scoring with clear CEM-Only Analysis badge
   - Component list with individual health grades

3. **Navigation** — Add Libraries link to the admin sidebar between existing nav items

## Acceptance Criteria

- Adding a CDN library (e.g. Shoelace) works end-to-end: add, resolve CEM, score, view dashboard
- Adding a local library with valid CEM path works
- Default hx-library is always present and cannot be deleted
- Existing health and mcp routes continue to work unchanged
- TypeScript compiles with zero errors
- Responsive layout, consistent with existing admin dark theme

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 8**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@apps/admin/src/app/libraries/`[id]/page.tsx:
- Around line 15-46: sourceLabel, sourceBadgeClass (and formatDate) are
duplicated between pages; extract them into a shared utility module and import
it to avoid duplication. Create a new module (e.g.,
apps/admin/src/lib/library-utils.ts) that exports sourceLabel, sourceBadgeClass,
and formatDate with the same signatures/types (use LibrarySource type from
library-registry), replace the local implementations in
apps/admin/src/app/libraries/[id]/page.tsx and
apps/admin/src/app/libraries/page.tsx with named imports from that module, and
update any callers to use the shared functions.

In `@apps/admin/src/app/libraries/page.tsx`:
- Around line 158-170: The label for the Name field is not associated with its
input; add a unique id to the input (e.g., id="library-name") and set the
label's htmlFor to that id so the <label> and <input> are programmatically
linked; update the input element that uses value={form.name} and onChange={(e)
=> update('name', e.target.value)} to include the id, and apply the same
id/htmlFor pattern to the other form fields so screen readers and click-to-focus
work correctly.
- Around line 147-153: The close button element that calls onClose currently
lacks an explicit type and will default to type="submit" inside forms; update
the button in apps/admin/src/app/libraries/page.tsx (the <button
onClick={onClose} ...> element) to include type="button" so clicking it won't
submit any surrounding form and retains its existing onClick/onClose behavior
and classes.
- Around line 526-532: The header and empty-state "Add Library" buttons are
missing an explicit type, causing them to default to "submit" in forms; update
the button elements that call setShowModal(true) (the header button rend

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: sourceLabel, sourceBadgeClass, and formatDate are duplicated verbatim between the two pages. DRY is a core standard and this is a straightforward extraction with no risk.

Accessibility is non-negotiable per the project mandate (healthcare, WCAG 2.1 AA). The modal backdrop is a static div with an onClick but no keyboard equivalent — Biome correctly flags noStaticElementInteractions and useKeyWithClickEvents. Adding role="dialog", aria-modal="true", and Escape key handling is required.
Real bug: the close button is inside the <form> element and without type="button" it defaults to type="submit", which would trigger form submission on click. Must fix.

WCAG 2.1 AA requirement — labels must be programmatically associated with their controls. Healthcare mandate. Biome flags noLabelWithoutControl. Affects screen reader users and click-to-focus. All form fields need htmlFor/id wiring.
Hardcoding gradeBadgeClass('A') is misleading — it implies a grade of A for any scored library. The grade isn't stored on LibraryEntry, so the honest display is neutral styling with "—".
Biome useButtonType lint error. All standalone buttons outside forms should still have explicit type="button" for correctness and lint compliance.
Same Biome useButtonType rule — the header "Add Library" and empty-state "Add Library" buttons need explicit type="button".
test-results/ is transient CI/local state and should not be tracked in git. Adding to .gitignore is correct hygiene.
